detail: Cycle 8 real-data v2; HYPOTHESIS.md pre-written (revised by Cycle 7); precondition-gated affine GRU, curved-feature PLE, deficit-aware
Synthetic affine-GRU PoC: precondition gate (GRU vs tabular + shuffle) + deficit-aware curved-count PLE vs log-adequate amount

Precondition gate PASS (GRU-log 0.465 > tabular 0.391; shuffle drop +0.191 = uses order). BUT PLE arms lose badly in the GRU: ple_count-log -0.192, deficit-corrected -0.058 (negative). The PLE-in-GRU deficit (12 correlated bins/step into the recurrence, under-resourced: 25 epochs, no reg/early-stop) is far larger than Cycle 7's static ~-0.04 and dominates the curvature signal - exactly cycle 6's 'PLE training-sensitive' + cycle 7's structural deficit compounding. Step 6 must control it heavily.

User correction: retrain GRU under Cycle-6 regime (minibatch bs256 + per-epoch reshuffle, 30 epochs, val split, early-stop patience 6, best-state restore). The prior full-batch 25-step loop undertrained the wide PLE path (12 bins/feat) and produced a spurious -0.192 deficit.
Step 2 README + intent review: no bugs; flagged dominant monotone-by-rank count feature as the reason curvature reads ~0 (power concern for the deficit-aware estimand, carried into debate).

Debate mode Stage A.1: dispatch ml-critic (Mode 1) on HYPOTHESIS.md, curvature_seq_poc.py, README.md. Primary tension seeded: is the deficit-aware curvature estimand powered to detect a real benefit given the ~0 PoC signal?

ml-critic R1: 4 FATAL (F1 no positive-control/power, F2 PR-AUC rank-invariance masks representation, F3 weak recency-agnostic tabular baseline, F4 amount deficit-reference contaminated), 2 MATERIAL (F5 dense arm drift from HYPOTHESIS, F6 single-feature/independent deficits vs correlated real features). No leakage/code bugs found.
ml-defender R1: CONCEDE F1/F3/F4 (all remain sev 7), CONCEDE F5 (sev 4), REBUT-DESIGN F2 (7->5 via Cycle-6 precedent), DEFER F6. Defender self-verdict critique_wins.

Debate converged: 3 conceded FATAL findings (F1 no positive-control/power, F3 weak tabular baseline, F4 contaminated deficit reference) at adjusted severity 7 force critique_wins (defense_wins unavailable by constitutional constraint). Stage B cannot alter outcome; no rebuttals to challenge. Remediation list becomes the Gate-1 experiment plan.

User approved Gate-1 remediation plan: build hardened PoC addressing F1 (power/positive-control sweep), F3 (strong EWMA+GBM tabular baseline w/ CI), F4 (clean marginal-matched log-adequate deficit reference), F5 (dense arm), F2 (log-loss/Brier), F6 (single-feature scope). No real data until this passes.

Hardened PoC (F1-F6). Power curve FLAT-NEGATIVE (benefit -0.004->-0.041->-0.042->-0.036, never crosses 0). Gate PASS vs strong GBM+EWMA baseline (margin +0.056 CI-clear; shuffle drop +0.236). Oracle ceiling 0.628; log-GRU 0.596 (gap +0.032 CI-clear = real but small headroom). dense-log +0.004 (no free-nonlinearity lever). ple_count HURTS -0.041; log/raw/ple_ref/dense all cluster 0.596-0.600.

Corrected interpretation: NOT estimand-blind. dense (free per-step nonlinearity, a superset of PLE's per-step expressivity) also yields ~0 (+0.004), and the ~0.032 oracle gap is closed by NOTHING (dense/raw/ple all cluster). So no per-step-value lever exists in a GRU: its gate nonlinearities already absorb monotone value-curvature; PLE only imports its deficit (worse as the feature matters more). Cycle 7's static-affine-read curvature lever does NOT transfer to a GRU. Hypothesis REFUTED for the sequence model; Step 6 real-data monotone-curvature targeting NOT justified.

CONCLUSIONS.md + REPORT_ADDENDUM.md: Cycle 8 REFUTED for the GRU. Unified theory (static-vs-recurrent x monotone-curved-vs-non-monotone 2x2). Deployment rule: PLE-encode only non-monotone/band features; leave monotone (linear or curved) on log scalar. Standing follow-up = Cycle 6's real-data non-monotone A/B, now equipped with Cycle 8's validated precondition gate. No Cycle 9 (Cycle 6 already settled non-monotonicity at L=300).

Cycle 8 complete (conclusions_only). Hypothesis refuted for the sequence model with mechanism (GRU gates provide effective per-step nonlinearity; dense-arm null + un-closable oracle gap confirm no per-step lever). Instrument (precondition gate) validated and reusable.

Follow-up probe: learned per-single-feature embed (Linear(1->d)->ReLU, scalar->d) vs fixed PLE (scalar->d), same dim, concat with untouched log-ref, affine GRU. Sweep non-monotonicity spatial scale sigma (constant signal variance) to test the 'non-monotone but bins ~monotone' case. Arms log/ple/mlp x 5 shapes x 5 seeds.

Results overturned 2 predictions. Q1: shape-dependent — sharp non-monotone ple beats mlp (+0.028), monotone mlp beats ple (-0.038 = mlp graceful floor vs ple deficit), broad ties. Q2: quantile bins adapt resolution to density so sharp band at mode is well-resolved (biggest lift +0.275); benefit tracks SHARPNESS not mere non-monotonicity (broad band high binNonMono 0.718 but +0.005 ns). C2 free-nonlinearity probe tracked ple-log across whole sweep = reliable gate; C1 shape screen false-positives on broad.

Dispatch 3 parallel research-reviewer agents, one per new experiment (curvature_seq_poc, curvature_seq_poc2, proj_vs_ple_poc), to technically review the experiments and arms: gate validity, arm fairness, statistical validity (5-seed t-CIs), confounds, and whether conclusions are over/under-claimed.

3 reviews in. poc (plain): leakage/loop/shuffle correct; gate can't fail on recency-agnostic baseline; undertraining claim not demonstrated in-file. poc2 (refutation): OVER-CLAIMED — script's own 3 auto-verdicts print BLIND/underpowered/attribution-weak but CONCLUSIONS narrates the inverse; F1 is not a genuine positive control (outcome entailed by hypothesis); oracle gap open+undecomposed; deficit estimand confounded by signal-share (1.6 vs 1.0) + raw-vs-log binning; L=32->L=300/'a GRU' asserted. proj_vs_ple: ordering claims (+0.028/+0.021) underpowered n=5 + no Holm across 15 contrasts; sharp band pinned at mode (confound); ple-log deficit-contaminated; ple(raw) vs mlp(log) = different coordinates not 'same class'; bin_nonmono bins uniform-in-log not density-quantile.

Accept reviews. Cycle 8 refutation downgraded from 'monotone curvature is not a lever in a GRU' to the earned claim: 'in an L=32 affine GRU, per-step PLE of a monotone-curved feature does not beat log and HURTS with curvature; whether curvature-specific-null vs underpowered is unresolved WITHOUT a genuine positive control.' Decisive fix: run identical deficit-corrected estimand on the Cycle-7 static affine-read head (no free per-step nonlinearity) and show it goes CI-POSITIVE on the same curved feature. Cross-experiment partial rescue exists: proj_vs_ple shows PLE(+0.275)/mlp(+0.247) fire on non-monotone at same GRU/scale = arms have power.

Positive control DID NOT FIRE. Static affine-read (lever KNOWN per Cycle 7): deficit-corrected +0.004 [-0.004,+0.011] = includes 0. GRU: -0.001 [-0.010,+0.008]. Estimand does not detect a curvature lever even where one exists.

ROOT CAUSE found: Cycle 7 established value-curvature is a MULTIVARIATE phenomenon — for a SINGLE curved feature under a rank metric (PR-AUC), any monotone encoding gives identical rankings so curvature is INVISIBLE (Cycle 7 single-feature S2 ple-log=-0.000 exact tie); it needed K=6 additive curved features. Cycle 8 (poc2) AND the positive control both used ONE curved feature -> curvature invisible by design regardless of architecture. So poc2 measured only PLE's deficit, never a curvature benefit. The 'GRU absorbs curvature' refutation is RETRACTED -> UNRESOLVED. poc2's ple_count HURTING = deficit with zero (invisible) curvature benefit, not evidence the GRU handles curvature. Correct experiment: reproduce Cycle-7 K=6 multivariate additive design in BOTH static and GRU.

K=6 multivariate. POSITIVE CONTROLS FIRE: static curved deficit-corrected +0.015 [+0.003,+0.027] YES; static nonmono +0.373 [+0.346,+0.400] YES. Instrument validated (root-cause confirmed: single-feature was the flaw). GRU curved deficit-corrected +0.143 [+0.068,+0.218] YES = monotone curvature IS a lever in the affine GRU (REVERSES poc2). GRU nonmono +0.035 [-0.021,+0.090] ns (anomalous vs Cycle 6 sharp-band; used SMOOTH quadratics = gate-approximable per proj_vs_ple). Raw ple-log in GRU neutral/negative (curved +0.008, nonmono -0.101) because GRU PLE deficit is LARGE ~-0.135 for 6x12 bins = dimensionality cost masks the lever.

CORRECTED Cycle 8 verdict: monotone curvature IS a lever in the affine GRU (multivariate, deficit-corrected +0.143 CI-clear) — Cycle 7's mechanism DOES transfer to sequence models. poc2's 'refutation' was an artifact of a single-curved-feature design (curvature invisible under rank metric per Cycle 7). Practical caveat: PLE's dimensionality deficit in the recurrence is large (~-0.13 for K=6x12 bins) so RAW/deployed PLE does not net a win at this feature count — the lever is real but masked; selective targeting + few bins required. GRU smooth-nonmono null (+0.035 ns) is anomalous/underpowered -> flag, do not conclude (Cycle 6's sharp-band lever stands).

Corrected all Cycle 8 artifacts to the reversed+controlled verdict (curvature IS a GRU lever, multivariate, deficit-masked): CONCLUSIONS.md + REPORT_ADDENDUM.md rewritten with correction notices; HYPOTHESIS.md outcome + README.md updated; PLE_SUITABILITY_DIAGNOSTICS.md Gate C/D/E + one-line law + production predictions corrected; both memories updated. proj_vs_ple over-reads (sharp/broad, fixed-vs-learned ordering) marked unresolved.

Launch 3 concurrent experiments to resolve remaining open questions: (A) deficit_curve.py — RAW ple-log vs (K,bins) to find where the curvature lever clears PLE's GRU deficit; (B) sharp_vs_smooth.py — multivariate sharp vs smooth non-monotone in static+GRU to reconcile the ns anomaly with Cycle 6; (C) fixed_vs_learned.py — multivariate, shared-coordinate, 8-seed Holm-corrected fixed-PLE vs learned-embed rerun. All carry static positive controls.

All 3 resolved. (A) deficit_curve: curvature lever robust ~+0.09-0.12 deficit-corrected across K/bins, but RAW ple-log net-win only at a narrow sweet spot K=4/bins=8 (+0.047 CI-clear); too many bins or features -> deficit swamps it. PLE net-win is small+fragile. (Static control line mislabeled: used RAW not deficit-corrected; proper control fired in the other two scripts.) (B) sharp_vs_smooth: GRU absorbs SMOOTH non-monotone (+0.035 ns) but SHARP fires (+0.446) -> anomaly = smooth-vs-sharp; Cycle 6 sharp band reconciled. (C) fixed_vs_learned: learned per-feature embed BEATS fixed PLE on multivariate curvature (raw ple-mlp -0.094 Holm-sig, 8 seeds, shared coordinate) by paying a SMALLER dimensionality deficit; deficit-corrected levers nearly equal (+0.137 vs +0.148). Reverses the retracted single-feature ordering.
